channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Reacher season 3 lands a February 2025 release date on Prime Video – and a teaser trailer that packs a literal punch for the titular hero
item.description: Prime Video's incredibly popular action series Reacher returns for its highly anticipated third season in early 2025.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Why Dion Harris believes Nvidia’s energy-intensive chips could actually help the climate
item.description: It takes a ton of energy to train the most advanced AI. According to one estimate, by the year 2027, AI electricity needs could be comparable to the energy use of all of Argentina.
item.dc:creator: Issie Lapowsky, Fast Company

item.title: Future exascale supercomputers will be built using these HPE Lego-like cabinets able to hold nearly 100,000 AMD EPYC Cores
item.description: Future Exascale supercomputers will be built using these HPE cabinets that can hold nearly 100,000 AMD Epyc Cores.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Struggling to hear TV dialogue? Try these simple fixes
item.description: If it sounds like actors on your TV are speaking in whispers, there's a way to fix it to make your TV audio sound better. The CyberGuy has several tips.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: 'Trump will be very good at' AI infrastructure
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How 2025’s digital products will evolve
item.description: When most people hear “TikTok features,” there’s usually some eye-rolling involved. But after 17 years of helping companies navigate digital transformation, I’ve noticed something more profound happening.
item.dc:creator: Brad Weber, Fast Company

item.title: Sick Americans say they’re turning to TikTok’s creator rewards program to crowdfund medical bills
item.description: “POV: you watched 3 times so my mom can pay for her medical bills,” reads one post on TikTok with more than 44 million views.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: IAEA chief: Iran is poised to 'quite dramatically' increase stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium
item.description: MANAMA, Bahrain (AP) — Iran is poised to “quite dramatically” increase its stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium as it has started cascades of advanced centrifuges, the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency warned Friday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How China’s export ban on these 3 materials could impact smartphones and national security
item.description: China has banned exports of key materials used to make a wide range of products, including smartphones, electric vehicles, radar systems and CT scanners, swiping back at Washington after it expanded export controls to include dozens of Chinese companies that make equipment used to produce advanced c
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Better together? Why AWS is unifying data analytics and AI services in SageMaker
item.description: Data warehousing, business intelligence, data analytics, and AI services are all coming together under one roof at Amazon Web Services.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The EU makes an urgent TikTok inquiry on Russia's role in Romanian election turmoil
item.description: The European Union has sent TikTok an urgent request for more information about Romanian intelligence files suggesting that Moscow coordinated influencers on its platform to promote an election candidate who became the surprise front-runner in the nation’s presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Future of SAP Architectures: A Hazy Outlook for Many Organizations
item.description: The future of SAP architectures is hybrid, but a survey by DSAG reveals that many organizations are undecided about their path forward.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What is a data scientist? A key data analytics role and a lucrative career
item.description: What is a data scientist? Data scientists are analytical data experts who use data science to discover insights from massive amounts of structured and unstructured data to help shape or meet specific business needs and goals.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: China's ban on key high-tech materials could have broad impact on industries, economy
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has banned exports of key materials used to make a wide range of products, including smartphones, electric vehicles, radar systems and CT scanners, swiping back at Washington after it expanded export controls to include dozens of Chinese companies that make equipment used to pro
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bitcoin $100K party: Why the price is a psychological milestone for longtime crypto believers
item.description: Crypto enthusiasts are rallying on social media over their new riches after Bitcoin crossed the $100,000 threshold on Thursday. “$100,000 is a psychological milestone,” entrepreneur and investor Anthony Pompliano wrote on X.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Michigan Democrats move to protect reproductive health data before GOP takes control of House
item.description: Democrats in Michigan are pressing to pass reproductive health care legislation before the party loses its majority with the new legislative session next year.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Volmert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: One Identity Named Winner of the Coveted Top InfoSec Innovator Awards for 2024
item.description: One Identity named Hot Company: Privileged Access Management (PAM) in 12th Cyber Defense Magazine’s Annual InfoSec Awards during CyberDefenseCon 2024.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: OpenAI unveils API for tracking OpenAI API usage, costs
item.description: OpenAI has introduced the Usage API, an API that allows enterprises to track their activity across the OpenAI API. With the Usage API, OpenAI API usage and costs can be assessed programmatically, the company said.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Breaking Down Data Silos with Intelligent Data Infrastructure for GenAI
item.description: Unlock the full potential of generative AI (genAI) with intelligent data infrastructure, streamlining data access and management, and enabling organizations to accelerate their genAI initiatives.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meta: AI created less than 1% of the disinformation around 2024 elections
item.description: AI-generated content accounted for less than 1% of the disinformation fact-checkers linked to political elections that took place worldwide in 2024, according to social media giant Meta.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cisco strengthens AWS integration to speed troubleshooting in multicloud environments
item.description: Cisco this week said it has bolstered integrations between its own hybrid cloud offerings and Amazon Web Services (AWS) to improve visibility and help enterprise customers more accurately troubleshoot application availability issues across multicloud networks.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The role of governance in AI: Building trust
item.description: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) is hot property when it comes to investment, but there’s a pronounced hesitancy around adoption. AI faces a fundamental trust challenge due to uncertainty over safety, reliability, transparency, bias, and ethics.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft: TPM 2.0 is a ‘non-negotiable’ requirement for Windows 11
item.description: With Windows 10 end of support on the horizon, Microsoft said its Trusted Platform Module (TPM) 2.0 requirement for PCs is a “non-negotiable standard” for upgrading to Windows 11.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Get started with Azure AI Content Understanding
item.description: Modern AI is about a lot more than chatbots, as shown by Microsoft’s Ignite 2024 pivot to using its stable of large and small language models to power autonomous agents.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Chinese online retailer Temu suspended in Vietnam
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Vietnam has suspended the operations of Chinese online retailer Temu after it failed to meet a government deadline to register the company by the end of November.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meta to build $10 billion AI data center in Louisiana as Elon Musk expands his Tennessee AI facility
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The largest artificial intelligence data center ever built by Facebook’s parent company Meta is coming to northeast Louisiana, the company said Wednesday, bringing hopes that the $10 billion facility will transform an economically neglected corner of the state. Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK and ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: High Impact Service Providers in 2025: The State of Federal CX
item.description: Explore what’s next for HISP agencies as they transform customer, employee and total experience The post High Impact Service Providers in 2025: The State of Federal CX first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: District of Columbia says Amazon secretly stopped fast deliveries to 2 predominantly Black ZIP codes
item.description: The District of Columbia sued Amazon on Wednesday, alleging the company secretly stopped providing its fastest delivery service to residents of two predominantly Black neighborhoods while still charging millions of dollars for a membership that promises the benefit.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman 'not that worried' about rival Elon Musk's influence in the Trump administration
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, who is in a legal dispute with rival Elon Musk, said he is “not that worried” about Musk's influence in the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kyndryl, Nokia expand AI, quantum-safe connectivity options
item.description: Kyndryl is extending its relationship with Nokia and expanding its menu of integration services by including more of Nokia’s data center networking products.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OECD: GenAI is affecting jobs previously thought safe from automation
item.description: According to a new report from the Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD), generative AI (genAI) will soon affect work areas previously considered to have a low likelihood of automation, according to The Register.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Report: AI expands the IT workforce as tech jobs return to offices
item.description: Artificial intelligence is driving growth for the IT workforce, and jobs in the technology sector are stabilizing as the market continues to show moderate growth amidst economic volatility, rapid hiring cycles, and public layoffs, according to a new study by IT staffing firm Motion Recruitment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Protecting Yourself from Malicious Apps on Google Play
item.description: Learn how to identify and avoid malicious apps on Google Play, including the SpyLoan malware, and protect yourself with antivirus software and cautious app permissions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AWS amplifies developer tools with new gen AI features
item.description: AWS is leaving no stone unturned to get generative AI tools embedded into every aspect of application development. At its annual re:Invent conference, AWS CEO Matt Garman showcased a large of number of features and tools that the company has built for developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to bulk delete spam and old text messages on iPhone
item.description: Tech wizard Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how to delete old unwanted texts and spam messages from cluttered inboxes of an iPhone in bulk.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Murder trial of tech consultant in stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee to go to jurors
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Closing arguments in the high-profile murder trial of a tech consultant charged in the stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee concluded Tuesday, with both sides urging jurors to use their common sense to deliver justice.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Founder of failed crypto lending platform Celsius Network pleads guilty to fraud charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The founder and former CEO of the failed cryptocurrency lending platform Celsius Network could face decades in prison after pleading guilty Tuesday to federal fraud charges, admitting that he misled customers about the business.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: AI weapons detection system used in Tennessee schools sued by FTC
item.description: The technology scans students and other visitors for weapons, using AI, without requiring them to remove items from their person, according to previous marketing.
item.dc:creator: Tori Gessner, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok Defends Election Integrity Measures in Romania Amid Controversy
item.description: TikTok removes election meddling networks, defends integrity measures to EU lawmakers amidst controversy over Romanian elections and Russian meddling.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN and STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: China bans exports to US of gallium, germanium, antimony in response to chip sanctions
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China announced Tuesday it is banning exports to the United States of gallium, germanium, antimony and other key high-tech materials with potential military applications, as a general principle, lashing back at U.S. limits on semiconductor-related exports.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: FTC opens Microsoft antitrust investigation that Trump administration must carry on or drop
item.description: Antitrust enforcers with the Federal Trade Commission have opened a wide-ranging investigation into Microsoft's business practices, starting a big legal project that an incoming Trump administration must take up or abandon.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4
